name: Lennie Niehaus
abstract: Leonard Niehaus (June 1, 1929 – May 28, 2020) was an American alto saxophonist, composer and arranger on the West Coast jazz scene. He played with the Stan Kenton Orchestra and served as one of Kenton's primary staff arrangers.

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: I suggested getting Lennie and Shelly Manne... The first session you did was in January and that was put out under Lennie’s name. I certainly respected Lennie enough to give him that title.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Poston
about_quotes.evidence: you could actually have them conduct this alumni band. So we put together a four-day event for the radio station... Pete Rugolo, Lennie Niehaus, Shorty Rogers, Marty Paich, you know Hank Levy — all the writers — Bill Russo, Bill Holman — everybody was still alive.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Poston interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Charles McPherson
about_quotes.evidence: The musical director for Clint Eastwood is ... Lennie Niehaus ... somebody recommended me, and Lenny got in touch with me. And I sent him a tape or something and he liked it and that was it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: Lennie Niehaus, yourself, Jimmy Giuffre, Shelly Manne on drums. Sounds like it could have been a real hip thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Jolly
about_quotes.evidence: And then Frank Rosolino was on Stan’s band and Lennie Niehaus and Bill Holman, Jack Montrose, the sax section
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Jolly interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: Lennie Niehaus is going to leave... Lennie’s decided to stay for a while.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999
